title: valerianella costata
intro: Valerianella costata, also known as sharp-fruited corn spurry, is a small annual flowering plant belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as diarrhea, as it contains anthraquinones, which have a laxative effect. The health benefits of Valerianella costata include its ability to soothe stomach cramps, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, allowing for a range of applications and dosages to support overall well-being.
